preview_text:
The model favors Australia at 54%, a meaningful edge over Egypt's 17% win probability. The underdog has less than one chance in five to take three points on a neutral ground.

The 29% draw probability sits between the two outright outcomes, suggesting a moderately competitive match with genuine two-way uncertainty. The model's spread—Australia leading by 36 percentage points across win and draw combined—indicates the favorite is expected to control possession and chances, though Egypt retains a plausible path to a result.

The model and market diverge sharply. The sportsbook consensus inverts the outcome, favoring Egypt at 38% while rating Australia at only 28%—a 26 percentage point gap on the win market. Polymarket reflects identical odds to the sportsbook consensus. The dashboard presents both signals without claiming to resolve their disagreement; traders and algorithm weight the available information differently.
